text: Takes function, traces computational graph, lowers StableHLO, compiles PJRT executable. Returns compiled executable along metadata needed execution.

title: Trace, lower, and compile a function to an XLA executable — compile_xla
code: compile_xla(   f,   args_flat,   in_tree,   donate = character(),   device = NULL,   arg_devices = list() )

title: Trace, lower, and compile a function to an XLA executable — compile_xla
text: f (function) Function compile. args_flat (list) Flat list abstract input values. in_tree (Node) Tree structure inputs. donate (character()) Names arguments whose buffers donated. device (NULL | character(1)) Target device (e.g. "cpu", "cuda"). NULL, inferred arg_devices traced arrays. arg_devices (list) Devices concrete (non-static) input arguments, extracted converting abstract values. Used together traced devices device inference device NULL.

title: Trace, lower, and compile a function to an XLA executable — compile_xla
text: list elements: exec: compiled PJRT executable. out_tree: output tree structure. const_arrays: Constants needed execution time. ambiguous_out: Logical vector indicating outputs ambiguous (NULL none ).

Compare AbstractArray Types — eq_type
text: Compare two abstract arrays type equality.

title: Compare AbstractArray Types — eq_type
code: eq_type(e1, e2, ambiguity)  neq_type(e1, e2, ambiguity)

title: Compare AbstractArray Types — eq_type
text: e1 (AbstractArray) First array compare. e2 (AbstractArray) Second array compare. ambiguity (logical(1)) Whether consider ambiguous field comparing. TRUE, arrays different ambiguity equal. FALSE, dtype shape compared.

title: Compare AbstractArray Types — eq_type
text: logical(1) - TRUE arrays equal, FALSE otherwise.

title: Compare AbstractArray Types — eq_type
code: a <- nv_aval("f32", c(2L, 3L)) b <- nv_aval("f32", c(2L, 3L))  # Same dtype and shape eq_type(a, b, ambiguity = FALSE) #> [1] TRUE  # Different dtype eq_type(a, nv_aval("i32", c(2L, 3L)), ambiguity = FALSE) #> [1] FALSE  # Different shape eq_type(a, nv_aval("f32", c(3L, 2L)), ambiguity = FALSE) #> [1] FALSE  # ambiguity parameter controls whether ambiguous field is compared c <- nv_aval("f32", c(2L, 3L), ambiguous = TRUE) eq_type(a, c, ambiguity = FALSE) #> [1] TRUE eq_type(a, c, ambiguity = TRUE) #> [1] FALSE  # neq_type is the negation of eq_type neq_type(a, b, ambiguity = FALSE) #> [1] FALSE

title: Filter List Node — filter_list_node

title: Filter List Node — filter_list_node
code: x <- list(a = 1, b = 2, c = 3) tree <- build_tree(x) sub <- filter_list_node(tree, c("a", "c")) tree_size(sub) #> [1] 2  unflatten(sub, x[c("a", "c")]) #> $a #> [1] 1 #>  #> $c #> [1] 3 #>

title: Gradient — gradient
text: Returns new function computes gradient f via reverse-mode automatic differentiation. f must return single float scalar. returned function signature f returns gradients structure inputs (subset selected wrt).

title: Gradient — gradient
text: f (function) Function differentiate. Arguments can arrayish (AnvlArray) static (non-array) values. Must return single scalar float array. wrt (character | integer | NULL) Names positions arguments compute gradient respect . arrayish (float array) arguments can included; static arguments must appear wrt. NULL (default), gradient computed respect arguments (must arrayish case).

title: Gradient — gradient
text: function

title: Gradient — gradient
code: f <- function(x, y) sum(x * y) g <- jit(gradient(f)) g(nv_array(c(1, 2), dtype = "f32"), nv_array(c(3, 4), dtype = "f32")) #> $x #> AnvlArray #>  3 #>  4 #> [ CPUf32{2} ]  #>  #> $y #> AnvlArray #>  1 #>  2 #> [ CPUf32{2} ]  #>   # Differentiate with respect to a single argument g_x <- jit(gradient(f, wrt = "x")) g_x(nv_array(c(1, 2), dtype = "f32"), nv_array(c(3, 4), dtype = "f32")) #> $x #> AnvlArray #>  3 #>  4 #> [ CPUf32{2} ]  #>   # Static (non-array) arguments are passed through but cannot be in wrt f2 <- function(x, power) sum(x^power) g2 <- jit(gradient(f2, wrt = "x"), static = "power") g2(nv_array(c(1, 2, 3), dtype = "f32"), power = 2L) #> $x #> AnvlArray #>  2 #>  4 #>  6 #> [ CPUf32{3} ]  #>

text: graph (AnvlGraph) Graph convert. unwrap (logical(1)) FALSE (default), output leaf wrapped AnvlArray "quickr" backend. TRUE, outputs returned plain R values. flat (logical(1)) FALSE (default), returned function takes structured top-level arguments matching formals traced function. TRUE, takes single flat list leaves (including static slots).

title: Convert an AnvlGraph to a quickr-compiled function — graph_to_quickr_function
text: returned function expects plain R scalars/vectors/arrays (AnvlArray) returns AnvlArray outputs default, plain R values unwrap = TRUE. graph returns multiple outputs (e.g. nested list), compiled function returns structure rebuilding output tree R. list supported primitives see vignette("primitives"). Supported dtypes f64, i32, pred. code generator currently supports arrays rank 5. primitives restricted (e.g. transpose currently handles rank-2 arrays). users prefer jit() backend = "quickr". function lower-level graph API.

JIT compile a function — jit
text: Wraps function traced compiled first call. Subsequent calls input structure, shapes, dtypes hit LRU cache skip recompilation. Unlike xla(), compiled executable created eagerly lazily first invocation.

title: JIT compile a function — jit
code: jit(   f,   static = character(),   cache_size = 100L,   backend = NULL,   device = NULL,   ... )

title: JIT compile a function — jit
text: f (function) Function compile. Must accept return AnvlArrays (/static arguments). static (character() | integer()) Names positions parameters f arrays. Static values embedded constants compiled program; new compilation triggered whenever static value changes. example useful want R control flow function. cache_size (integer(1)) Maximum number compiled executables keep LRU cache. backend (NULL |  character(1)) Compilation backend (e.g. "xla", "quickr"). special value "auto" defers backend selection call-time. NULL (default) respects device otherwise falls back default_backend(). device (NULL | character(1) | nv_device | device_arg()) Target device. concrete device specified, arrays moved . default (NULL) infers device call time, falling back default_device(). order use dynamic device selection "auto" backend (e.g. functions without dynamic inputs constant creation), set device = device_arg("<arg>"). ... Backend-specific options. Passing option supported selected backend raises error. See XLA JIT arguments Quickr JIT arguments sections options accepted backend.

title: JIT compile a function — jit
text: JitFunction (function formals f). returned wrapper expects AnvlArray inputs returns AnvlArray values.

title: JIT compile a function — jit
text: various ways specify device backend use. Concrete backend: case fix concrete backend (backend "auto"), device can inferred set explicitly. Setting device explicitly allows enforce function always uses specified device, e.g. "cuda:0". device argument set, encountered arrays copied . device specified (NULL; default) device inferred input arrays constants within program. conflicting devices found, error thrown. array device found, fall back default device. Auto backend: setting backend = "auto", backend inferred array inputs otherwise fall back default backend. want jit() function without array inputs make work different devices, set device = device_arg("<argname>") <argname> name argument specifying device. Note necessary "auto" backend. using concrete backend, can just specify device via static argument.

title: JIT compile a function — jit
text: donate (character(), default character()): names arguments whose underlying buffers may donated (.e., reused/consumed ) compiled XLA executable. Donated buffers must used caller call; can reduce memory usage copies large inputs. Must overlap static.

title: JIT compile a function — jit
text: unwrap (logical(1), default FALSE): TRUE, compiled function returns plain R arrays instead AnvlArrays. Useful jitted function's output consumed non-anvl R code extra wrapping get stripped .

title: JIT compile a function — jit
code: f <- jit(function(x, y) x + y) f(nv_array(1), nv_array(2)) #> AnvlArray #>  3 #> [ CPUf32{1} ]   # Static arguments enable data-dependent control flow g <- jit(function(x, flag) {   if (flag) x + 1 else x * 2 }, static = "flag") g(nv_array(3), TRUE) #> AnvlArray #>  4 #> [ CPUf32{1} ]  g(nv_array(3), FALSE) #> AnvlArray #>  6 #> [ CPUf32{1} ]  with_backend("quickr", {   h <- jit(function(x, y) x + y)   h(nv_array(1), nv_array(2)) }) #> AnvlArray #> [1] 3 #> [ CPUf64{1} ]

title: Create a Primitive — new_primitive

Inside fn, primitive accessible via lexically-bound symbol self (AnvlPrimitive); pass first argument graph_desc_add(). subgraphs (character()) Names parameters subgraphs (higher-order primitives). static (character() | integer()) Passed jit(). device (NULL | character(1) | device_arg()) Passed jit(). Useful primitives array inputs (e.g. prim_fill) device must come explicit argument. register (logical(1)) TRUE (default), register result name primitive registry.

what: Combine arrays by rows or columns — nv_bind
title: Combine arrays by rows or columns — nv_bind
text: Combine arrays along row (nv_rbind) column (nv_cbind) dimension. Arguments first promoted common data type (see nv_promote_to_common()). input handled according rank: 0-D: broadcast match non-stacked dimensions inputs. 1-D: treated single row/column. : used -.

title: Combine arrays by rows or columns — nv_bind
code: nv_rbind(...)  nv_cbind(...)  # S3 method for class 'AnvlBox' rbind(..., deparse.level = 1)  # S3 method for class 'AnvlArray' rbind(..., deparse.level = 1)  # S3 method for class 'AnvlBox' cbind(..., deparse.level = 1)  # S3 method for class 'AnvlArray' cbind(..., deparse.level = 1)

title: Combine arrays by rows or columns — nv_bind
text: ... (arrayish) Arrays combine. Inputs promoted common data type. deparse.level Ignored. Kept compatibility base::rbind() base::cbind().

title: Combine arrays by rows or columns — nv_bind
text: arrayish

title: Combine arrays by rows or columns — nv_bind
text: base::rbind() base::cbind() applied array() rank > 2 flatten trailing dimensions column axis (c(2, 3, 4) array becomes 2 x 12 matrix). nv_rbind nv_cbind instead preserve non-stacked dimensions: combining two c(2, 3, 4) arrays nv_rbind produces c(4, 3, 4) array, nv_cbind c(2, 6, 4) array.

title: Combine arrays by rows or columns — nv_bind
code: # Vectors as rows / columns nv_rbind(nv_array(1:3), nv_array(4:6)) #> AnvlArray #>  1 2 3 #>  4 5 6 #> [ CPUi32{2,3} ]  nv_cbind(nv_array(1:3), nv_array(4:6)) #> AnvlArray #>  1 4 #>  2 5 #>  3 6 #> [ CPUi32{3,2} ]   # Scalar broadcasting nv_rbind(nv_matrix(1:6, nrow = 2), nv_scalar(0)) #> AnvlArray #>  1 3 5 #>  2 4 6 #>  0 0 0 #> [ CPUf32{3,3} ]   # Rank-3 arrays preserve trailing dimensions a <- nv_array(1:24, shape = c(2, 3, 4)) shape(nv_rbind(a, a)) # c(4, 3, 4) #> [1] 4 3 4

text: Broadcast scalar arrays match shape non-scalar arrays. non-scalar arrays must shape.

title: Broadcast Scalars to Common Shape — nv_broadcast_scalars
Broadcast Scalars to Common Shape — nv_broadcast_scalars
text: ... (arrayish) Arrays broadcast. Scalars broadcast common non-scalar shape.

title: Broadcast Scalars to Common Shape — nv_broadcast_scalars
text: (list() arrayish) List broadcasted arrays.

title: Broadcast Scalars to Common Shape — nv_broadcast_scalars
code: x <- nv_array(c(1, 2, 3)) # scalar 1 is broadcast to shape [3] nv_broadcast_scalars(x, nv_scalar(1)) #> [[1]] #> AnvlArray #>  1 #>  2 #>  3 #> [ CPUf32{3} ]  #>  #> [[2]] #> AnvlArray #>  1 #>  1 #>  1 #> [ CPUf32{3} ]  #>

title: Cholesky Decomposition — nv_chol
text: Computes Cholesky decomposition symmetric positive-definite matrix. Supports batched inputs: dimensions last two batch dimensions.

title: Cholesky Decomposition — nv_chol
text: operand (arrayish) Symmetric positive-definite matrix least 2 dimensions. last two dimensions form square matrix; leading dimensions batch dimensions. lower (logical(1)) FALSE (default, matching base R's base::chol()), compute upper triangular factor U operand = t(U) %*% U. TRUE, compute lower triangular factor L operand = L %*% t(L).

title: Cholesky Decomposition — nv_chol
text: arrayish Triangular matrix shape data type input.

title: Cumulative Maximum — nv_cummax
text: Running maximum, optionally along single dimension.

title: Cumulative Maximum — nv_cummax
text: operand (arrayish) Operand. dim (integer(1) | NULL) Dimension along accumulate. NULL (default), input first flattened 1-D array, like base::cummax(). with_indices (logical(1)) FALSE (default), returns running-maximum array. TRUE, returns list(values = ..., indices = ...) indices 1-based index last occurrence running maximum position (dtype i32, matching torch). dim = NULL, indices refer flattened input.

title: Cumulative Maximum — nv_cummax
text: arrayish (with_indices = FALSE) named list two arrays (with_indices = TRUE).

title: Cumulative Maximum — nv_cummax
text: nv_cummax() (dim = NULL) base::cummax() flatten multi-dimensional input 1-D accumulating, flatten order differs: anvl arrays row-major (C order), flattened sequence iterates last dim fastest, whereas base R uses column-major (Fortran) order. two agree 1-D inputs.

title: Cumulative Maximum — nv_cummax
code: x <- nv_matrix(c(3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9), nrow = 2) nv_cummax(x) #> AnvlArray #>  3 #>  4 #>  5 #>  5 #>  5 #>  9 #> [ CPUf32{6} ]  nv_cummax(x, dim = 1L) #> AnvlArray #>  3 4 5 #>  3 4 9 #> [ CPUf32{2,3} ]  nv_cummax(x, dim = 1L, with_indices = TRUE) #> $values #> AnvlArray #>  3 4 5 #>  3 4 9 #> [ CPUf32{2,3} ]  #>  #> $indices #> AnvlArray #>  1 1 1 #>  1 1 2 #> [ CPUi32{2,3} ]  #>

title: Determinant — nv_det
text: Computes determinant square matrix via nv_determinant().

title: Determinant — nv_det
text: operand (arrayish) Square matrix floating-point data type.

title: Determinant — nv_det
text: Scalar arrayish dtype operand.

title: Determinant — nv_det
code: a <- nv_matrix(c(4, 3, 6, 3), nrow = 2, dtype = "f64") nv_det(a) #> AnvlArray #>  -6 #> [ CPUf64{} ]

title: Determinant in modulus/sign form — nv_determinant
text: Computes determinant square matrix modulus / sign decomposition matching base R's base::determinant(). plain scalar determinant, use nv_det().

title: Determinant in modulus/sign form — nv_determinant
text: operand (arrayish) Square matrix floating-point data type. logarithm (logical(1)) TRUE (default, matching base R), modulus log(abs(det(operand))). FALSE, modulus abs(det(operand)).

title: Determinant in modulus/sign form — nv_determinant
text: Named list elements modulus sign, scalar arrayish dtype operand. full determinant sign * exp(modulus) (logarithm = TRUE) sign * modulus (logarithm = FALSE).

title: Determinant in modulus/sign form — nv_determinant
text: computing determinant, use: $$P = L U$$ $$\det(L) = 1$$ $$\det() = \det(U) / \det(P) = \mathrm{sign}(P^{-1}) \, \prod_i U_{ii}   = \mathrm{sign}(P) \, \prod_i U_{ii}$$ Matching base R's det_ge_real, magnitude computed log space logarithm = TRUE (\(\sum_i \log|U_{ii}|\)) direct product logarithm = FALSE (\(\prod_i |U_{ii}|\)).

title: Determinant in modulus/sign form — nv_determinant
code: a <- nv_matrix(c(4, 3, 6, 3), nrow = 2, dtype = "f64") nv_determinant(a) #> $modulus #> AnvlArray #>  1.7918 #> [ CPUf64{} ]  #>  #> $sign #> AnvlArray #>  -1 #> [ CPUf64{} ]  #>  nv_determinant(a, logarithm = FALSE) #> $modulus #> AnvlArray #>  6 #> [ CPUf64{} ]  #>  #> $sign #> AnvlArray #>  -1 #> [ CPUf64{} ]  #>

title: Create a Device — nv_device
text: Constructs backend-specific device object. device identifies compute resources, CPU, specific GPU. relevant data allocation (e.g. via nv_array()) also compilation (jit).

title: Create a Device — nv_device
text: x (character(1) | device object) Identifier device (e.g. "cpu", "cuda", "cuda:<n>"), existing device object (returned -). backend (NULL | character(1)) backend create device. Defaults default_backend() NULL.

title: Create a Device — nv_device
text: backend-specific device object (e.g. PJRTDevice "xla", quickr_device "quickr").

title: Create a Device — nv_device
code: # Create CPU device for xla backend: nv_device("cpu", "xla") #> <CpuDevice(id=0)> # Create CPU device for quickr backend: nv_device("cpu", "quickr") #> QuickrDevice(cpu)  # Pass through an existing device: dev <- nv_device("cpu") identical(nv_device(dev), dev) #> [1] TRUE

title: Symmetric Eigendecomposition — nv_eigh
text: Computes eigendecomposition symmetric matrix operand shape (n, n): $$= \mathrm{vectors} \, \mathrm{diag}(\mathrm{values}) \,   \mathrm{vectors}^\top.$$ lower triangle operand read. columns vectors (orthonormal) eigenvectors values length-n vector (real) eigenvalues ascending order. Output names order match base::eigen().

title: Symmetric Eigendecomposition — nv_eigh
text: operand (arrayish) Symmetric square matrix floating-point data type.

title: Symmetric Eigendecomposition — nv_eigh
text: Named list elements values (length n) vectors (shape (n, n)). dtype input.

title: Symmetric Eigendecomposition — nv_eigh
code: x <- nv_matrix(c(2, 1, 1, 2), nrow = 2, dtype = "f64") nv_eigh(x) #> $values #> AnvlArray #>  1 #>  3 #> [ CPUf64{2} ]  #>  #> $vectors #> AnvlArray #>  -0.7071  0.7071 #>   0.7071  0.7071 #> [ CPUf64{2,2} ]  #>

title: LU Decomposition — nv_lu
text: Computes partial-pivoted LU decomposition matrix operand: $$P = L U,$$ \(P\) permutation matrix, \(L\) unit lower triangular, \(U\) upper triangular. function returns L U separate matrices. Use prim_lu() get packed LU form.

text: operand (arrayish) Matrix data type floating-point exactly 2 dimensions.

title: LU Decomposition — nv_lu
text: Named list: L – unit lower-triangular factor shape (m, k), (m, n) = shape(operand) k = min(m, n). U – upper-triangular factor shape (k, n). pivots – length k, dtype i32. LAPACK-style sequential 1-based row swaps returned getrf. permutation – length m, dtype i32. 1-based permutation vector representing \(P\).

title: QR Decomposition — nv_qr
text: Computes reduced QR decomposition matrix operand: $$= Q R,$$ \(Q\) orthonormal columns (\(Q^\top Q = \)) \(R\) upper triangular. \(m \times n\) input \(k = \min(m, n)\), \(Q\) shape \(m \times k\) \(R\) shape \(k \times n\).

title: QR Decomposition — nv_qr
text: operand (arrayish) Matrix data type floating-point exactly 2 dimensions.

title: QR Decomposition — nv_qr
text: Named list elements Q (shape (m, k)) R (shape (k, n)), (m, n) = shape(operand) k = min(m, n). data type operand.

title: Quantile — nv_quantile
text: Computes probs quantile(s) array along dimension. probs follows scalar-vs-array convention nv_select()'s index: length-1 numeric (e.g. 0.5) treats probs scalar — output dim removed, like reduction; 1-D R array (e.g. array(c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75))) prepends leading dimension size length(probs). Plain length-K (K > 1) vectors rejected; wrap array() make array intent explicit.

title: Quantile — nv_quantile
text: x (arrayish) array. probs (numeric(1) | 1-D array) One probabilities [0, 1]. Either length-1 numeric (scalar; dim dropped) 1-D array (leading dim size length(probs) prepended). Plain length-K (K > 1) vectors rejected — wrap array(). dim (integer(1) | NULL) Dimension along compute quantile. NULL (default), uses last dimension. interpolation (character(1)) One "linear" (default), "lower", "higher", "nearest", "midpoint". See "Interpolation modes".

title: Quantile — nv_quantile
text: arrayish scalar probs: shape x dim removed. array probs: leading dimension size length(probs) prepended.

title: Quantile — nv_quantile
text: Let h = (n - 1) * q 0-based fractional index axis length n probability q, lo = floor(h), hi = ceil(h), frac = h - lo. : "linear" (default): (1 - frac) * sorted[lo] + frac * sorted[hi]. "lower": sorted[lo] — lower bracket linear. "higher": sorted[hi] — upper bracket linear. "nearest": sorted[lo] frac < 0.5 else sorted[hi]. "midpoint": (sorted[lo] + sorted[hi]) / 2.

title: Quantile — nv_quantile
code: x <- nv_array(c(3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 2, 6)) nv_quantile(x, 0.5) # = nv_median(x) #> AnvlArray #>  3.5000 #> [ CPUf32{} ]  nv_quantile(x, array(c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75))) #> AnvlArray #>  1.7500 #>  3.5000 #>  5.2500 #> [ CPUf32{3} ]  nv_quantile(x, 0.5, interpolation = "lower") #> AnvlArray #>  3 #> [ CPUf32{} ]

title: Sequence — nv_seq
text: Creates 1-D array values start end (inclusive). Without steps, behaves like R's seq(start, end) producing integer values. steps, produces steps evenly spaced values (like seq(start, end, length.= steps)). nv_seq_like() variant dtype, ambiguous, device default like.

title: Sequence — nv_seq
code: nv_seq(   start,   end,   steps = NULL,   dtype = NULL,   ambiguous = FALSE,   device = NULL )  nv_seq_like(   like,   start,   end,   steps = NULL,   dtype = NULL,   ambiguous = NULL,   device = NULL )

title: Sequence — nv_seq
text: start, end (numeric(1)) Start end values. steps NULL, must satisfy start <= end. steps (integer(1) NULL) Number evenly spaced values generate. Must least 1. NULL (default), generates consecutive integer values start end. dtype (character(1)) Data type. Default "i32" steps NULL, "f32" steps given. nv_seq_like(), NULL uses dtype(like). ambiguous (logical(1)) Whether type ambiguous. Ambiguous types usually arise R literals (e.g., 1L, 1.0) follow special promotion rules. See vignette("type-promotion") details. device ( character(1) | PJRTDevice | quickr_device | NULL) Device data live . like (AnvlArray) Existing array whose attributes used defaults (nv_seq_like()).

title: Sequence — nv_seq
text: arrayish 1-D array length end - start + 1.

title: Sequence — nv_seq
code: nv_seq(3, 7) #> AnvlArray #>  3 #>  4 #>  5 #>  6 #>  7 #> [ CPUi32{5} ]  x <- nv_array(c(1, 2, 3), dtype = "f64") nv_seq_like(x, 1, 5) #> AnvlArray #>  1 #>  2 #>  3 #>  4 #>  5 #> [ CPUf64{5} ]

title: Singular Value Decomposition — nv_svd
text: Computes reduced ("economy") singular value decomposition matrix operand shape (m, n): $$= u \, \mathrm{diag}(d) \, vt,$$ u orthonormal columns, vt orthonormal rows, d length-k (k = min(m, n)) vector non-negative singular values descending order. Note: unlike base::svd(), returns right singular vectors v shape (n, k) (= u %*% diag(d) %*% t(v)), primitive returns already transposed vt shape (k, n) (matching underlying LAPACK / cuSOLVER output avoiding extra transpose). CUDA backend primitive currently requires m >= n (cuSOLVER's gesvd restriction). host (LAPACK) backend supports shape.

title: Singular Value Decomposition — nv_svd
text: operand (arrayish) Matrix data type floating-point exactly 2 dimensions.

title: Singular Value Decomposition — nv_svd
text: Named list elements d (length k), u (shape (m, k)), vt (shape (k, n)). dtype input.

title: Triangular Solve — nv_triangular_solve
text: Solves triangular system linear equations. left_side = TRUE, returns x op() %*% x = b. left_side = FALSE, returns x x %*% op() = b. op t() depending transpose_a.

title: Triangular Solve — nv_triangular_solve
code: nv_triangular_solve(   a,   b,   left_side = TRUE,   lower = TRUE,   unit_diagonal = FALSE,   transpose_a = FALSE )

title: Triangular Solve — nv_triangular_solve
text: (arrayish) Triangular coefficient matrix least 2 dimensions. last two dimensions must equal; leading dimensions batch dimensions. b (arrayish) Right-hand side. shape (B..., n, n), b may either: full rank — shape (B..., n, k) left_side = TRUE, (B..., k, n) left_side = FALSE; one rank less, shape (B..., n), meaning single column (left_side = TRUE) row (left_side = FALSE) per batch — reshaped internally reshape undone result output rank matches b. b's batch dimensions (B...) must match 's exactly. left_side (logical(1)) TRUE (default), solve op() %*% x = b; FALSE, solve x %*% op() = b. lower (logical(1)) Whether lower upper triangular. Defaults TRUE. unit_diagonal (logical(1)) TRUE, diagonal treated ones (actual values diagonal ignored). Defaults FALSE. transpose_a (logical(1)) TRUE, solve t() place . Defaults FALSE.

title: Triangular Solve — nv_triangular_solve
text: arrayish solution x, shape dtype b.

title: Triangular Solve — nv_triangular_solve
text: convenience, b may one fewer dimension (single right-hand side per batch, shape (B..., n) shape (B..., n, n)). reshaped internally column (left_side = TRUE) row (left_side = FALSE) reshaped back way . broadcast, ambiguous (NumPy).

title: Triangular Solve — nv_triangular_solve
code: L <- nv_matrix(c(2, 1, 0, 3), nrow = 2, dtype = "f32") b <- nv_matrix(c(4, 3), nrow = 2, dtype = "f32") nv_triangular_solve(L, b) #> AnvlArray #>  2.0000 #>  0.3333 #> [ CPUf32{2,1} ]

title: Primitive Gather — prim_gather
text: Gathers slices operand array positions specified start_indices. index vector start_indices identifies starting position operand, slice size slice_sizes extracted position. gathered slices assembled output array. inverse prim_scatter(): gather reads slices array given indices, scatter writes slices array given indices.

title: Primitive Gather — prim_gather
code: prim_gather(   operand,   start_indices,   slice_sizes,   offset_dims,   collapsed_slice_dims,   operand_batching_dims,   start_indices_batching_dims,   start_index_map,   index_vector_dim,   indices_are_sorted = FALSE,   unique_indices = FALSE )

title: Primitive Gather — prim_gather
text: operand (arrayish) Arrayish value data type. start_indices (arrayish integer type) Array starting indices. Contains index vectors map positions operand via start_index_map. dimension specified index_vector_dim holds index vectors. slice_sizes (integer()) Size slice gather operand dimension. Must length equal ndims(operand). offset_dims (integer()) Dimensions output correspond non-collapsed slice dimensions operand. collapsed_slice_dims (integer()) Dimensions operand collapsed (removed) slice. corresponding entries slice_sizes must 1. Together offset_dims operand_batching_dims, must account dimensions operand. operand_batching_dims (integer()) Dimensions operand batch dimensions. Use integer(0) batch dimensions. start_indices_batching_dims (integer()) Dimensions start_indices correspond batch dimensions. Must length operand_batching_dims. start_index_map (integer()) Maps component index vector operand dimension. example, start_index_map = c(1L) means index vector indexes first dimension operand. index_vector_dim (integer(1)) Dimension start_indices contains index vectors. set ndims(start_indices) + 1, scalar element start_indices treated length-1 index vector. indices_are_sorted (logical(1)) Whether indices guaranteed sorted. Setting TRUE may improve performance produces undefined behavior indices actually sorted. Default FALSE. unique_indices (logical(1)) Whether indices guaranteed unique (duplicates). Setting TRUE may improve performance produces undefined behavior indices actually unique. Default FALSE.

title: Primitive Gather — prim_gather
text: arrayish data type operand. output shape composed offset dimensions (slice) remaining dimensions start_indices. See underluing stableHLO function details.

text: Start indices clamped slice extracted: clamp(1, start_index, nv_shape(operand) - slice_sizes + 1). means --bounds indices cause error, effective start position may differ requested one.

text: stablehlo quickr reverse

title: Primitive LU Decomposition — prim_lu
text: Computes partial-pivoted LU decomposition matrix operand: $$P = L U,$$ \(P\) permutation matrix, \(L\) unit lower triangular, \(U\) upper triangular. L (implicit unit diagonal) U packed single LU output matching LAPACK's getrf layout. \(P\) returned two equivalent forms: pivots (LAPACK's sequential row-swap encoding) permutation (explicit permutation vector).

title: Primitive LU Decomposition — prim_lu
text: operand (arrayish) Matrix data type floating-point exactly 2 dimensions.

title: Primitive LU Decomposition — prim_lu
text: list three arrayish values: LU (m, n) dtype input; pivots (k,) dtype i32 k = min(m, n) (1-based row swaps row exchanged row pivots[] elimination step ); permutation (m,) dtype i32, 1-based permutation vector \(P\) (P %*% )[, ] equals [permutation[], ].

title: Primitive LU Decomposition — prim_lu
text: stablehlo

title: Primitive LU Decomposition — prim_lu
text: Lowers "lu" stablehlo::hlo_custom_call() (backed LAPACK CPU cuSOLVER CUDA) LU pivots, followed stablehlo::hlo_while() loop converts pivots permutation -graph.

title: Primitive LU Decomposition — prim_lu

title: Primitive QR Decomposition — prim_qr
text: Computes reduced QR decomposition matrix operand: $$= Q R,$$ \(Q\) orthonormal columns (\(Q^\top Q = \)) \(R\) upper triangular. \(m \times n\) input \(k = \min(m, n)\), \(Q\) shape \(m \times k\) \(R\) shape \(k \times n\).

title: Primitive QR Decomposition — prim_qr
text: operand (arrayish) Matrix data type floating-point exactly 2 dimensions.

title: Primitive QR Decomposition — prim_qr
text: Named list elements Q (shape (m, k)) R (shape (k, n)), (m, n) = shape(operand) k = min(m, n). data type operand.

title: Primitive QR Decomposition — prim_qr
text: stablehlo

title: Primitive QR Decomposition — prim_qr
text: Lowers "geqrf" + "orgqr" stablehlo::hlo_custom_call() pair (backed LAPACK CPU cuSOLVER CUDA) + postprocessing.

Must data type operand neutral element w.r.t. reductor. dims (integer()) Dimensions reduce . drop (logical(1)) TRUE (default) reduced dimensions removed; FALSE kept size 1. reductor (function(lhs, rhs)) Binary reducer producing scalar dtype operand. title: Primitive Scatter — prim_scatter
text: Produces result array identical input except slices positions specified scatter_indices updated values update array. multiple indices point location, update_computation function determines combine values (default new value replaces old one). inverse prim_gather(): gather reads slices array given indices, scatter writes slices array given indices.

title: Primitive Scatter — prim_scatter
code: prim_scatter(   input,   scatter_indices,   update,   update_window_dims,   inserted_window_dims,   input_batching_dims,   scatter_indices_batching_dims,   scatter_dims_to_operand_dims,   index_vector_dim,   indices_are_sorted = FALSE,   unique_indices = FALSE,   update_computation = NULL )

title: Primitive Scatter — prim_scatter
text: input (arrayish) Arrayish value data type. base array scatter . scatter_indices (arrayish integer type) Array indices. Contains index vectors map positions input via scatter_dims_to_operand_dims. dimension specified index_vector_dim holds index vectors. update (arrayish) Update values array. Must data type input. update_window_dims (integer()) Dimensions update window dimensions, .e. correspond slice written input. inserted_window_dims (integer()) Dimensions input whose slices size 1 inserted (present) update window. Together update_window_dims input_batching_dims, must account dimensions input. input_batching_dims (integer()) Dimensions input batch dimensions. Use integer(0) batch dimensions. scatter_indices_batching_dims (integer()) Dimensions scatter_indices correspond batch dimensions. Must length input_batching_dims. scatter_dims_to_operand_dims (integer()) Maps component index vector input dimension. example, scatter_dims_to_operand_dims = c(1L) means index vector indexes first dimension input. index_vector_dim (integer(1)) Dimension scatter_indices contains index vectors. set ndims(scatter_indices) + 1, scalar element scatter_indices treated length-1 index vector. indices_are_sorted (logical(1)) Whether indices guaranteed sorted. Setting TRUE may improve performance produces undefined behavior indices actually sorted. Default FALSE. unique_indices (logical(1)) Whether indices guaranteed unique (duplicates). Setting TRUE may improve performance produces undefined behavior indices actually unique. Default FALSE. update_computation (function) Binary function f(old, new) combines existing value input value update. default (NULL) uses function(old, new) new, replaces old value.

title: Primitive Scatter — prim_scatter
text: arrayish data type shape input. ambiguous input ambiguous.

title: Primitive Sort — prim_sort
text: Sorts arrays along given dimension. Sorting determined first operand : sort key, additional operands reordered permutation sorts first. enables idioms like argsort (sort x paired iota read second output) key-value sorts (sort keys paired values). operands must shape; dtypes may differ. 1-dimensional slices along dim sorted independently; dimensions preserved.

title: Primitive Sort — prim_sort
code: prim_sort(operands, dim = 1L, descending = FALSE, is_stable = FALSE)

title: Primitive Sort — prim_sort
text: operands (list arrayish) One arrays sort. first sort key; rest carried along permutation. must share shape. dim (integer(1)) Dimension along sort. descending (logical(1)) TRUE, sort key descending order (largest first). Default FALSE. Additional operands reordered permutation regardless. is_stable (logical(1)) TRUE, sort stable: relative order equal keys preserved. Default FALSE.

title: Primitive Sort — prim_sort
text: list arrayish One sorted output per element operands, order. output shape, data type, ambiguity corresponding input.

title: Primitive Sort — prim_sort
text: stablehlo reverse

title: Primitive Sort — prim_sort
text: Lowers stablehlo::hlo_sort() comparator uses stablehlo::hlo_compare() (LT ascending, GT descending) first operand.

title: Primitive Sort — prim_sort
code: x <- nv_array(c(3, 1, 4, 1, 5)) prim_sort(list(x), dim = 1L)[[1L]] #> AnvlArray #>  1 #>  1 #>  3 #>  4 #>  5 #> [ CPUf32{5} ]   # Sort indices by the values (argsort): pair x with iota and read off # the second result. idx <- nv_iota(dim = 1L, dtype = "i64", shape = 5L) out <- prim_sort(list(x, idx), dim = 1L) out[[1L]] # sorted x #> AnvlArray #>  1 #>  1 #>  3 #>  4 #>  5 #> [ CPUf32{5} ]  out[[2L]] # permutation indices #> AnvlArray #>  2 #>  4 #>  1 #>  3 #>  5 #> [ CPUi64{5} ]

title: Primitive Static Slice — prim_static_slice
text: Extracts slice array using static (compile-time) indices. indices, limits, strides fixed R integers. Use prim_dynamic_slice() instead start position must computed runtime (e.g. depends array values).

title: Primitive Static Slice — prim_static_slice
text: operand (arrayish) Arrayish value data type. start_indices (integer()) Start indices (inclusive), one per dimension. Must satisfy 1 <= start_indices <= limit_indices per dimension. limit_indices (integer()) End indices (inclusive), one per dimension. Must satisfy limit_indices <= nv_shape(operand) per dimension. strides (integer()) Step sizes, one per dimension. Must >= 1. stride 1 selects every element; stride 2 selects every element, etc.

title: Primitive Static Slice — prim_static_slice
text: arrayish data type input shape ceiling((limit_indices - start_indices + 1) / strides). ambiguous input ambiguous.

title: Primitive Static Slice — prim_static_slice
text: stablehlo quickr reverse

title: Primitive Static Slice — prim_static_slice
text: Lowers stablehlo::hlo_slice().

title: Primitive Static Slice — prim_static_slice
code: # 1-D: extract elements 2 through 4 (limit is exclusive) x <- nv_array(1:10) prim_static_slice(x, start_indices = 2L, limit_indices = 5L, strides = 1L) #> AnvlArray #>  2 #>  3 #>  4 #>  5 #> [ CPUi32{4} ]   # 1-D: every other element using strides x <- nv_array(1:10) prim_static_slice(x, start_indices = 1L, limit_indices = 10L, strides = 2L) #> AnvlArray #>  1 #>  3 #>  5 #>  7 #>  9 #> [ CPUi32{5} ]   # 2-D: extract a submatrix (rows 1-2, columns 2-3) x <- nv_matrix(1:12, nrow = 3, ncol = 4) prim_static_slice(x,   start_indices = c(1L, 2L),   limit_indices = c(3L, 4L),   strides       = c(1L, 1L) ) #> AnvlArray #>   4  7 10 #>   5  8 11 #>   6  9 12 #> [ CPUi32{3,3} ]

title: Primitive Singular Value Decomposition — prim_svd
text: Computes reduced ("economy") singular value decomposition matrix operand shape (m, n): $$= u \, \mathrm{diag}(d) \, vt,$$ u orthonormal columns, vt orthonormal rows, d length-k (k = min(m, n)) vector non-negative singular values descending order. Note: unlike base::svd(), returns right singular vectors v shape (n, k) (= u %*% diag(d) %*% t(v)), primitive returns already transposed vt shape (k, n) (matching underlying LAPACK / cuSOLVER output avoiding extra transpose). CUDA backend primitive currently requires m >= n (cuSOLVER's gesvd restriction). host (LAPACK) backend supports shape.

title: Primitive Singular Value Decomposition — prim_svd
text: operand (arrayish) Matrix data type floating-point exactly 2 dimensions.

title: Primitive Singular Value Decomposition — prim_svd
text: Named list elements d (length k), u (shape (m, k)), vt (shape (k, n)). dtype input.

title: Primitive Singular Value Decomposition — prim_svd
text: stablehlo

title: Primitive Singular Value Decomposition — prim_svd

title: Primitive Triangular Solve — prim_triangular_solve
text: Solves system linear equations triangular coefficient matrix. left_side TRUE, solves op() %*% x = b x. left_side FALSE, solves x %*% op() = b x. Dimensions last two batch dimensions must match b (broadcasting). op ^T depending transpose_a.

title: Primitive Triangular Solve — prim_triangular_solve

title: Primitive Triangular Solve — prim_triangular_solve
text: (arrayish) Triangular coefficient matrix data type floating-point least 2 dimensions. last two dimensions must equal (square matrix); leading dimensions batch dimensions. b (arrayish) Right-hand side. data type rank (rank >= 2), matching leading batch dimensions. size 's last two (square) dimensions must equal b's second--last dimension left_side = TRUE, b's last dimension left_side = FALSE. left_side (logical(1)) TRUE, solve op() %*% x = b. FALSE, solve x %*% op() = b. lower (logical(1)) TRUE, lower triangular. FALSE, upper triangular. unit_diagonal (logical(1)) TRUE, assume diagonal elements 1. transpose_a (logical(1)) TRUE, solve t() place . Defaults FALSE.

title: Lower a graph to StableHLO — stablehlo
text: Converts traced AnvlGraph StableHLO intermediate representation (IR). graph operation translated corresponding StableHLO op. result can serialized MLIR text via stablehlo::repr() subsequently compiled XLA executable pjrt::pjrt_compile(). rules translating stablehlo stored $rules[["stablehlo"]] primitives. low-level function; users use jit() xla() instead.

title: Lower a graph to StableHLO — stablehlo
text: graph (AnvlGraph) graph lower (e.g. produced trace_fn()). constants_as_inputs (logical(1)) TRUE (default), constants registered inputs StableHLO function can passed execution time. FALSE, added inputs. Set FALSE closures. Note GraphLiterals always inlined StableHLO function. env (HloEnv | NULL) Optional environment reusing variable mappings across nested function lowerings (e.g. higher-order primitives like nv_while). donate (character()) Names arguments whose buffers donated. Donated buffers can aliased outputs type, enabling -place operations.

title: Lower a graph to StableHLO — stablehlo

title: Lower a graph to StableHLO — stablehlo
code: x <- nv_array(c(1, 2)) graph <- trace_fn(function(y) y + x, list(y = nv_aval("f32", shape = c()))) graph #> <AnvlGraph> #>   Inputs: #>     %x1: f32[] #>   Constants: #>     %c1: f32[2] #>   Body: #>     %1: f32[2] = broadcast_in_dim [shape = 2, broadcast_dimensions = <any>] (%x1) #>     %2: f32[2] = add(%1, %c1) #>   Outputs: #>     %2: f32[2]  stablehlo(graph) #> [[1]] #> func.func @main (%0: tensor<2xf32>, %1: tensor<f32>) -> tensor<2xf32> { #> %2 = "stablehlo.broadcast_in_dim" (%1) { #> broadcast_dimensions = array<i64> #> }: (tensor<f32>) -> (tensor<2xf32>) #> %3 = stablehlo.add %2, %0 : tensor<2xf32> #> return %3 : tensor<2xf32> #> } #>  #> [[2]] #> [[2]][[1]] #> GraphValue(ConcreteArray(f32, (2)))  #>  #>

what: Trace an R function into a Graph — trace_fn
title: Trace an R function into a Graph — trace_fn
text: Executes f abstract array arguments records every primitive operation AnvlGraph. resulting graph can lowered StableHLO (via stablehlo()) transformed (e.g. via transform_gradient()).

title: Trace an R function into a Graph — trace_fn
code: trace_fn(   f,   args = NULL,   desc = NULL,   mode = NULL,   args_flat = NULL,   in_tree = NULL )

title: Trace an R function into a Graph — trace_fn
text: f (function) function trace. Must JitFunction (.e. already jitted). args (list (AnvlArray | AbstractArray)) (unflattened) arguments function. Mutually exclusive args_flat/in_tree pair. desc (NULL | GraphDescriptor) Optional descriptor. NULL (default), new descriptor created. mode (character(1)) handle inputs. Options : "toplevel": Used jit(). Default. "subgraph": Use tracing subgraphs higher-order primitives like prim_while(). "inline": Use transformations like jit, graph later inlined parent graph. args_flat (list) Flattened arguments. Must accompanied in_tree. in_tree (Node) Tree structure describing args_flat maps back f's arguments.

title: Trace an R function into a Graph — trace_fn
text: AnvlGraph containing traced operations.

title: Trace an R function into a Graph — trace_fn
code: graph <- trace_fn(function(x, y) x + y,   args = list(x = nv_array(1, dtype = "f32"), y = nv_array(2, dtype = "f32"))) graph #> <AnvlGraph> #>   Inputs: #>     %x1: f32[1] #>     %x2: f32[1] #>   Body: #>     %1: f32[1] = add(%x1, %x2) #>   Outputs: #>     %1: f32[1]

title: anvl (development version)
text: Renamed user-facing API functions match base R names: nv_sine() -> nv_sin(), nv_cosine() -> nv_cos(), nv_ceil() -> nv_ceiling(), nv_cholesky() -> nv_chol(). corresponding primitives renamed step: prim_sine() -> prim_sin(), prim_cosine() -> prim_cos(), prim_cholesky() -> prim_chol(). nv_reduce_mean() renamed nv_mean(). nv_solve() longer requires symmetric positive-definite uses LU instead Cholesky decomposition. , longer differentiable, reverse rule LU implemented yet. nv_chol() / prim_chol() now default lower = FALSE (upper-triangular factor), matching base R’s chol(). Previously defaulted lower = TRUE.

title: anvl (development version)
text: New matrix-decomposition primitives corresponding nv_*() functions: qr, lu, svd, eigh. None implement reverse rule yet. nv_triangular_solve() (wraps already-existing prim_triangular_solve()). nv_det() nv_determinant(). latter can also called via determinant() generic. nv_inv(), can also called via solve(operand) (missing second argument). qr, chol, solve base R now dispatch nv_qr(), nv_chol(), nv_solve() AnvlArray / AnvlBox inputs.

title: anvl (development version)
text: New unary primitives corresponding nv_*() functions: acos, acosh, asin, asinh, atan, atanh, cosh, sinh, digamma, lgamma, polygamma, erf, erf_inv, erfc. New API functions nv_mod() (flooring remainder) nv_trunc() (truncation toward zero).

title: anvl (development version)
text: New primitives corresponding nv_*() functions: cumsum, cumprod, cummax, cummin. prim_cumprod() yet reverse rule.

title: anvl (development version)
text: New primitives prim_sort(), prim_top_k(), prim_reduce(), prim_argmax(), prim_argmin(). nv_sort() / nv_argsort() – sort along dimension, return permutation . nv_top_k() – k largest values along dimension. nv_median() / nv_quantile() – median / quantiles along dimension. median() dispatches nv_median(). nv_argmax() / nv_argmin() – index maximum / minimum along dimension (ties broken smallest index). nv_select() – select slice along dimension index.

title: anvl (development version)
text: nv_array() gained byrow argument fills array R object row-major order, mirroring matrix(byrow = TRUE) (#165). New nv_matrix(data, nrow, ncol, ...) works like R’s matrix(). New API functions nv_rbind() nv_cbind() corresponding rbind() / cbind() generics. New API function nv_flatten() flattening 1-D.

title: anvl (development version)
text: New AnvlArray -> R vector converters: .numeric(), .double(), .integer(), .logical(), .vector(). New function await() blocks underlying computation finished. New tree utilities map_tree() pmap_tree() applying functions leaf-wise (possibly nested) lists. mean() median() now error called na.rm = TRUE, since anvl arrays carry NAs. mean() also rejects non-zero trim.

title: anvl (development version)
text: nv_reduce_sum(), nv_reduce_prod(), nv_reduce_max(), nv_reduce_min(), nv_reduce_any(), nv_reduce_all() nv_mean() now default dims = NULL, reduces dimensions returns scalar. Previously, dims required.

title: anvl (development version)
text: overloaded %% operator now calls new nv_mod() consistent base R. reverse rule prim_reduce_prod() longer produces NaN / Inf gradients input contains zeros. CI now actually runs torch-comparison tests. nv_runif() properly respects lower argument. overloaded %% operator now calls new nv_mod() consistent base R. reverse rule prim_reduce_prod() longer produces NaN/Inf gradients input contains zeros. CI now actually runs torch-comparison tests

title: anvl 0.2.0
text: package renamed anvil anvl avoid conflict Bioconductor package AnVIL. AnvilTensor/nv_tensor renamed AnvlArray nv_array line R’s array(). Also, nv_aten() renamed nv_aval(). Subsetting list() (e.g. x[list(1, 3)]) longer supported. Use array() wrap indices instead, e.g. x[array(c(1L, 3L))]. mirrors input convention used everywhere else package. Removed debug mode. Remove NSE support nvl_if. now requires passing 0-argument closures true false arguments. Primitives renamed nvl_* prim_*. underlying primitive object containing rules metadata now part JitPrimitive function via primitive attribute.

title: anvl 0.2.0
text: Better composability: jit()ted functions can now used jit()-calls. mechanism underlying new eager mode. Eager mode added: means, can now nv_add(1, nv_array(1:2)) actually perform computation type inference. experimental {quickr} backend added runs CPU now supports subset available operations. can enable via backend argument jit() nv_array() via anvl.default_backend option. nvl_cholesky() compute Cholesky decomposition matrix. nvl_triangular_solve() solve system linear equations triangular matrix. nv_diag() create diagonal matrix 1-D tensor. nv_eye() create identity matrix. nv_solve() solve system linear equations. nv_cholesky() compute Cholesky decomposition matrix. nv_device() constructs backend-specific device object (e.g. nv_device("cpu")) can passed device array constructors like nv_fill() nv_iota(). nv_crossprod() nv_tcrossprod() matrix cross-products. nv_outer() outer product. nv_extract_diag() extract diagonal matrix. nv_trace() compute trace matrix. nv_tril() nv_triu() extract lower/upper triangular parts. nv_squeeze() nv_unsqueeze() drop add length-1 dimensions. nv_log2() nv_log10(). nv_is_infinite() nv_is_nan(). nv_sd() nv_var() standard deviation variance. jit() now accepts integer positions static argument. New S3 methods dim(), nrow(), ncol(), length() anvl arrays. Printing tensors via nv_print() now also works GPUs. R vectors length 1 arrays now auto-converted passed jitted functions. Improved device handling jit()

title: anvl 0.2.0
text: Many operations now done asynchronously, improves performance, especially GPUs.

title: anvl 0.2.0
text: +-Inf/NaN correctly created f64 inlined XLA exectuable (#182). caused wrong results e.g. nv_reduce_max() working f64. Corrected argument checks nv_iota(). Fix check wrt arguments gradient() must floats. nv_subset() nv_subset_assign() now error trailing-comma subscripts (#273).

title: anvl 0.2.0
text: New vignette implementing Gaussian Processes. New vignette implementing Metropolis-Hastings sampling.

title: anvl 0.2.0
text: installation guide added. Linux ARM now supported (CPU ). use CUDA backend, now possible install cuda12.8 package (see installation guide), requires compatible CUDA driver.

title: anvl 0.1.0
text: Initial release
